Trend Scout Daily Intelligence — May 6, 2026

🔥 Top 5 "Wow + Money" Overlap Projects

1. Agentic Skills Frameworks (claude-skills / mattpocock-skills / obra-superpowers)

  • Trendshift: #1 with 177.5K stars (agentic skills framework), mattpocock skills at 57.6K, taste-skill at 15.1K
  • HN signal: Addy Osmani "Agent Skills" still resonating (78 pts yesterday), now Cloudflare announces agents can create accounts + deploy autonomously
  • TrustMRR overlap: DM Champ ($187K MRR, AI selling agent) — direct commercial application of agentic AI
  • Why winning: Skills/craft systems are becoming the "package.json for AI agents." The framework wars are here.

2. AI Voice & Accent Tech

  • HN #2: Telus uses AI to alter call-agent accents (49 pts, climbing)
  • TrustMRR: DM Champ ($187K), Bookedin ($48K MRR, AI receptionists), Interactive Video SaaS ($45K)
  • Pattern: Voice AI is monetizing hard while staying under the hype radar. Accent alteration = ethical minefield but massive demand.

3. SEO/AEO Automation

  • TrustMRR: SEOBOT ($67K MRR, +14%), AEO Engine ($52K MRR, +1%), SEO Stack ($65K MRR), LocalRank ($49K), Launch Club ($50K — Reddit SEO for AI)
  • Trendshift: Short video engine (10.1K stars) — content automation feeds SEO
  • Why winning: LLM search optimization is the new SEO gold rush. Every company now asks "how do I show up in ChatGPT?"

4. AI Sales Outreach & Automation

  • TrustMRR: Dealsourcr ($158K, +29%), Prosp ($128K), Virlo ($47K, +32%), Upscale System ($53K, +42%), AdStellar ($39K, +41%)
  • HN: "Computer Use is 45x more expensive than structured APIs" (346 pts) — validates that structured API approaches (like these sales tools) beat raw computer-use agents
  • Why winning: Cold outreach is being automated end-to-end. Structured API beats general AI every time on cost.

5. Health Tech / Telehealth

  • TrustMRR: TrimRx ($246K, +28%), Kibu ($234K, stable), Hormone prescribing ($152K, +5%)
  • Trendshift: No direct overlap but healthcare compliance tools steady
  • Why winning: GLP-1 demand is a mega-trend. Kibu serves I/DD with EHR — deeply niche, deeply profitable.

💰 Top 5 Money-Makers Nobody's Talking About

1. 1Capture — $229K MRR (+9%)

"Double your trial-to-paid conversion without making product changes." FOR SALE. Pure B2B conversion tool. Zero hype, pure utility.

2. Stealth Venture (Simon) — $186K MRR (+5%)

Complete black box. $186K growing 5% monthly. Likely a niche B2B tool that found a single profitable wedge.

3. DataExpert / TechCreator — $61K MRR (+2%)

Data engineering education + creator economy play. Zach Wilson built a media company disguised as education.

4. Private Venture (Reddit GEO/LLM Agency) — $50K MRR, 75% margin

B2B SaaS Reddit marketing specifically for LLM/GEO visibility. $500K asking price, 100% inbound. Zero sales process. The agency model for AI search optimization is printing.

5. Lunchbreak — $46K MRR (+9%)

AI content detection evasion. Ethically gray but clearly in demand. FOR SALE. Students + professionals paying to make AI output undetectable.

🚀 Top 5 Trending with No Monetization

1. CLAUDE.md (Karpathy-inspired) — 110K stars ⭐

Single file to improve Claude Code behavior. Zero monetization. Pure utility. Could become a paid template marketplace or consulting vehicle.

2. Agent Orchestration Platform — 39.8K stars

"Leading agent orchestration platform for Claude." Multi-agent swarms, enterprise architecture. Open-source now but enterprise licensing obvious next step.

3. Knowledge Graph Explorer — 11.1K stars

"Graphs that teach > graphs that impress." Turn codebases into interactive knowledge graphs. Works with Claude Code, Codex, Cursor. Zero monetization but RAG-as-a-service is obvious.

4. .de DNSSEC Outage — 561 pts on HN

Not a project but a signal: DNS infrastructure fragility is top-of-mind. Opportunity for DNS monitoring/backup tools.

5. Gemma 4 Multi-Token Prediction — 484 pts on HN

Google's faster inference with multi-token drafters. Open model acceleration. The inference optimization layer is where money will flow.

📊 Key Patterns

Pattern 1: Structured APIs > Computer Use Agents

HN's #4 story (346 pts) proves what the market already knows: "Computer Use is 45x more expensive than structured APIs." The winning AI products (Dealsourcr, SEOBOT, Prosp) all use structured approaches, not general-purpose agents.

Pattern 2: Skills/Craft Layer is the New Package Manager

Claude skills (177.5K stars), mattpocock skills (57.6K), taste-skill (15.1K), DESIGN.md brand systems (70.8K). The "skills" abstraction for AI agents is becoming standardized. This is 2024's "prompt engineering" — everyone needs a skills layer.

Pattern 3: Accent/Voice AI Enters Mainstream

Telus altering agent accents hit HN. DM Champ and Bookedin monetizing conversational AI. The uncanny valley is closing — voice AI is now commercially viable at scale, but ethics debates are heating up.

Pattern 4: LLM Search Optimization = New SEO Industry

AEO Engine, SEOBOT, SEO Stack, Launch Club, LocalRank, Private Venture (Reddit GEO agency). At least 6 companies in TrustMRR are directly targeting "how to appear in AI search." This is a $100M+ emerging market.

Pattern 5: Open-Source AI Design Tools Exploding

Local-first Claude Design alternative (19.8K stars), DESIGN.md brand systems (70.8K), knowledge graph explorer (11.1K). The "AI-assisted design" category is splitting into open-source (community) vs. commercial (Figma, etc.).

💡 Quick Takes

1. The "agent economy" infrastructure is being built RIGHT NOW. Cloudflare's announcement that agents can create accounts, buy domains, and deploy means autonomous agents are becoming first-class economic actors. The skills/framework layer (177K stars) is the developer tooling for this economy.

2. Computer Use is a trap. At 45x the cost of structured APIs, the economics only work for demos. The real money is in API-first AI tools — which is exactly what's dominating TrustMRR's revenue charts.

3. Voice AI ethics will be 2026's content moderation debate. Telus accent alteration is the opening shot. DM Champ ($187K) and Bookedin ($48K) show the market doesn't care about ethics debates — money flows first, regulation follows.
